label: CD8 T cell
evidence: The cell_type_annotations.json file lists 'CD8 T cell' as a fine-grained annotation with fetal scope. In the atlas paper (DOI: 10.1038/s41586-024-08002-x), T cells including CD8+ T cells emerge in prenatal skin later in gestation, accompanying thymus formation from around 10 PCW. The adult counterpart cluster is annotated as 'Tc' (Cytotoxic T cell / CD8+ T cell). Single-cell TCR sequencing was performed to resolve T cell subsets (Supplementary Tables 2 and 3). Dhariwala et al. (2020) provide detailed CyTOF phenotyping showing CD8+ T cells in fetal skin are predominantly naive with a subset showing memory-like features.
